source_jobs.description: <div class="x_ms-outlook-mobile-reference-message x_skipProofing"><strong>Director of Sales – Oil, Gas &amp; Chemicals (Fenceline Solutions)</strong><br><br><strong>Location:</strong>&nbsp;Houston, TX (Preferred) | Remote – Gulf Coast Region<br><strong>Reports To:</strong>&nbsp;Chief Commercial Officer<br><br><strong>About Picarro</strong><br><br>Picarro is a global leader in precision measurement technology, helping organizations transform how they detect, measure, and manage emissions. Our advanced analytics, laser-based sensing technology, and cloud platform enable industrial operators to identify emissions earlier, reduce operational risk, improve compliance, and achieve sustainability goals.<br><br>We’re expanding our commercial team to accelerate growth in the oil, gas, and chemicals market.<br><br><strong>The Opportunity</strong><br><br>This is a rare opportunity to help build a new market category.<br><br>As Director of Sales, you will lead business development efforts for Picarro’s Fenceline Solutions across North America’s refining and chemical sectors. You’ll be responsible for developing new customer relationships, building pipeline, and closing strategic enterprise opportunities with some of the industry’s largest operators.<br><br>This is a true hunter role. Success requires someone who enjoys creating opportunities, navigating complex organizations, and partnering with customers to solve operational and environmental challenges.<br><br><strong>What You’ll Do</strong><br><br>Develop and execute a territory strategy targeting major refining and chemical companies.<br>Identify, qualify, and close new enterprise opportunities from prospecting through contract execution.<br>Build trusted relationships with HSSE, Operations, Environmental, Engineering, Reliability, and executive leadership.<br>Drive complex, consultative sales cycles involving multiple stakeholders.<br>Deliver accurate pipeline forecasting and maintain CRM discipline.<br>Partner closely with Product, Marketing, Technical Solutions, and Customer Success to deliver exceptional customer outcomes.<br>Represent Picarro at industry conferences, customer sites, and technical events.<br>Provide market intelligence and customer feedback to influence product strategy and go-to-market initiatives.<br><br><strong>What We’re Looking For</strong><br><br><strong>Required Qualifications</strong><br><br>10+ years of successful enterprise B2B sales experience.<br>Proven track record selling complex technical solutions into the refining, petrochemical, or chemical industries.<br>Demonstrated success in developing new business and opening strategic accounts.<br>Experience managing long, consultative sales cycles involving multiple decision makers.<br>Ability to engage executive leadership while maintaining credibility with plant and operational teams.<br>Strong territory planning, forecasting, and pipeline management skills.<br>Willingness to travel throughout North America (approximately 40–50%).<br><br><strong>Preferred Qualifications</strong><br><br>Experience selling one or more of the following:<br><br>Industrial software or SaaS<br>Environmental monitoring solutions<br>Continuous emissions monitoring systems (CEMS)<br>LDAR technologies<br>Industrial automation or process controls<br>Industrial IoT platforms<br>Asset performance management solutions<br>Environmental compliance technologies<br><br>Knowledge of EPA environmental regulations, refinery operations, or chemical manufacturing is highly desirable but

Senior Manager, Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A)
source_jobs.description: <p><strong>Senior Manager, Financial Planning &amp; Analysis (FP&amp;A)<br><br>Location: Santa Clara, CA (On-site)</strong><br><br>At Picarro, we are revolutionizing the way industries and governments monitor air quality, emissions, and energy infrastructure. As the world leader in high-precision scientific instruments and monitoring solutions, our technology helps solve some of the most pressing global challenges.<br><br>We are seeking a Senior Manager, Financial Planning &amp; Analysis (FP&amp;A) to support strategic financial planning and operational decision-making across the business. This role reports to the Head of Global FP&amp;A and serves as a player-coach—combining hands-on financial analysis with leadership of FP&amp;A analysts. The Senior Manager will partner closely with leadership across finance, operations, and go-to-market teams to deliver insights that drive performance, improve forecasting accuracy, and support company growth.<br><br>Responsibilities<br><br>• Lead core financial planning processes, including annual budgeting, quarterly forecasting, and long-range financial planning<br>• Develop and maintain financial models supporting revenue forecasting, operating expenses, headcount planning, and cash flow projections<br>• Conduct scenario modeling and sensitivity analysis to support strategic decision-making<br>• Partner with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Operations leadership to support go-to-market strategy, pricing decisions, and investment planning<br>• Analyze business performance, including pipeline trends, sales performance, and go-to-market ROI, to guide strategic growth decisions<br>• Prepare executive-level reporting, including variance analysis, KPI dashboards, performance metrics, and board presentation materials<br>• Lead monthly business reviews and communicate key drivers behind financial performance, risks, and opportunities<br>• Collaborate with Accounting, Operations, and Supply Chain teams to ensure financial forecasts align with operational plans<br>• Drive improvements in financial planning processes, reporting automation, and financial systems integration<br>• Support strategic initiatives, including product launches, market expansion, and operational scaling<br>• Mentor and develop FP&amp;A team members while remaining actively involved in financial modeling and analysis<br><br>Qualifications<br><br>• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business Administration, or related field<br>• 7–10+ years of progressive experience in FP&amp;A, corporate finance, or business finance within an operating company<br>• Experience managing or mentoring finance professionals while remaining hands-on with financial modeling and analysis<br>• Advanced financial modeling, budgeting, forecasting, and business performance analysis experience<br>• Advanced Excel skills, including complex modeling and scenario analysis<br>• Experience with ERP and FP&amp;A tools such as NetSuite, Adaptive Planning, or similar systems<br>• Ability to translate complex financial data into clear insights for senior leadership<br>• Strong collaboration and communication skills with the ability to partner cross-functionally across global teams<br>• High level of integrity and discretion when handling sensitive financial and investor data<br><br>Preferred Experience<br><br>• Experience supporting hardware, manufacturing, or technology companies with global operations<br>• Experience with product-based businesses that combine hardware, software, and services, revenue models<br>• Strong understanding of SaaS and hybrid hardware/SaaS metrics, including ARR, CAC, LTV, and revenue forecasting<br>• MBA, CPA, or CMA preferred<br>&
